results.text: Stunned for 1 second, then recover automatically
Stunned for one second. Every second after that, roll vs. unmodified Will to snap out of it
Stunned for one second. Every second after that, roll vs. Will, plus whatever bonuses or penalties you had on your original roll, to snap out of it.
Stunned for [[1d6]] seconds. Every second after that, roll vs. modified Will, as above, to snap out of it
Stunned for [[2d6]] seconds. Every second after that, roll vs. modified Will, as above, to snap out of it.

Awe causes (25 - Will) seconds of ecstasy. Roll vs. Will each second to recover; see Incapacitating Conditions (p. 428). Depending on the circumstances, this may be merely inconvenient, or humiliating.
Acquire a new mental quirk. Awe inspires quirks that reflect admiration.

Stunned for [[1d6]] seconds. Every second after that, roll vs. modified Will, as above, to snap out of it. And acquire a new mental quirk that reflects admiration (see Quirks, p. 162). This is the only way to acquire more than five quirks.
Awe causes [[1d6]] minutes of ecstasy, then roll vs. Will each minute to recover.
Awe causes [[2d6]] minutes of ecstasy, then roll vs. Will each minute to recover.
Awe causes [[4d6]] minutes of ecstasy, then roll vs. Will each minute to recover.

Awe causes you to worship at the feet of the one who awed you – you must obey his every command as if you had Slave Mentality! This lasts [[3d6]] minutes then roll vs. Will once per minute to snap out of it.

Acquire a -10-point mental disadvantage. Awe might impel you to adopt one of your new idol’s self-imposed mental disadvantages (see p. B121) out of solidarity, turn you into his servant (Reprogrammable), or make you feel inferior (Low Self-Image).

Acquire a -10-point mental disadvantage. Awe might impel you to adopt one of your new idol’s self-imposed mental disadvantages (see p. B121) out of solidarity, turn you into his servant (Reprogrammable), or make you feel inferior (Low Self-Image). If you already have a -5 to -10-point disadvantage that could result from Awe or Confusion, it worsens to a -15 trait.

Experience ecstasy for [[1d6]] minutes. Acquire a new -10-point mental disadvantage. Awe might impel you to adopt one of your new idol’s self-imposed mental disadvantages (see p. B121) out of solidarity, turn you into his servant (Reprogrammable), or make you feel inferior (Low Self-Image).

Experience ecstasy for [[2d6]] minutes. Acquire a new -10-point mental disadvantage. Awe might impel you to adopt one of your new idol’s self-imposed mental disadvantages (see p. B121) out of solidarity, turn you into his servant (Reprogrammable), or make you feel inferior (Low Self-Image).

Experience ecstasy for [[4d6]] minutes. Acquire a new -10-point mental disadvantage. Awe might impel you to adopt one of your new idol’s self-imposed mental disadvantages (see p. B121) out of solidarity, turn you into his servant (Reprogrammable), or make you feel inferior (Low Self-Image).

Awe overcomes you. You immediately collapse in a helpless, ecstatic fit that lasts [[1d6]] minutes and costs [[1d6]] FP. After that time, roll vs. Will each minute to recover. Any critical failure costs you 1 Will permanently.  Should you survive your first reaction, roll vs. Will to recover. If you fail, the GM rolls for another insane action, and so on.
Acquire a -15-point mental disadvantage. Awe usually results in Fanaticism – either for the one who awed you or his cause.

Acquire a -20-point mental disadvantage. Awe might impel you to adopt one of your new idol’s self-imposed mental disadvantages (see p. B121) out of solidarity, turn you into his servant (Reprogrammable), or make you feel inferior (Low Self-Image).

Acquire a -30-point mental disadvantage. Awe might impel you to adopt one of your new idol’s self-imposed mental disadvantages (see p. B121) out of solidarity, turn you into his servant (Reprogrammable), or make you feel inferior (Low Self-Image). To make up -30 points, the GM may have to select more than one trait.

Awe causes [[1d6]] minutes of ecstasy, then roll vs. Will each minute to recover. Acquire a -15-point mental disadvantage. Awe usually results in Fanaticism – either for the one who awed you or his cause.

Awe causes [[2d6]] minutes of ecstasy, then roll vs. Will each minute to recover. Acquire a -15-point mental disadvantage. Awe usually results in Fanaticism – either for the one who awed you or his cause.

Awe causes [[2d6]] minutes of ecstasy, then roll vs. Will each minute to recover. Acquire a -15-point mental disadvantage. Awe usually results in Fanaticism – either for the one who awed you or his cause. Permanently lose 1 point of Will.
